summary: Validate strategic terrain recovery before M7 content-lock reconsideration
acceptance_criteria: Prove the default player-facing map no longer uses the rejected drawing or polygon-first spatial-index direction.
Prove terrain patches, barriers/channels, strategic nodes, route corridors, and district governance footprints are distinct in data, interaction, explanation, and screenshots.
Prove hit testing starts from actionable terrain-route-node layers and reaches governance overlays only after node/corridor/blocker/terrain priorities.
Prove route preview and AI reasons use the same query model and expose estimated days, capacity, season, terrain, bottleneck, blocker, unknown state, risk, and governance consequences when relevant.

Include negative tests for disguised grid/lattice/cell leakage, visual-neighbor-without-route, visually-distant-explicit-route, barrier-without-crossing, governance-adjacent-but-route-blocked, geometry perturbation without path change, and renderer-only fake route lines.
Produce screenshot/manual-smoke evidence at 1440x900 and 1280x720 with clean console output and player-visible terrain/node/corridor/overlay explanation.
After acceptance and main integration, reopen M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 for product-owner reconsideration; do not approve content lock or start M7-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 autonomously.
required_tests: focused strategic terrain validation tests including query parity, hit-test priority, and anti-grid/lattice/cell leakage tests
manual-smoke notes with screenshots and console-clean assertions
notes: This validation blocks formal M7 content lock after the strategic terrain rejection.
It must not start M7-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 or claim content lock approval.
If historical claims appear during validation, route them to historical_researcher instead of treating them as accepted facts.
next_action: Await renderer/interaction acceptance before validation starts.
